title: Amoora tsangii
intro: Amoora tsangii, also known as Tsang's Amoora, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Chinese herbalism, due to its purported health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Amoora tsangii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Amoora tsangii is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions including fever, digestive issues, and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Amoora tsangii belongs to the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. This family comprises about 500 species of trees and shrubs, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Amoora consists of about 30 species, which are native to Asia and Australia. These plants are typically small to medium-sized trees with heart-shaped leaves and small, greenish-yellow flowers. Amoora tsangii is one of the lesser-known species within this genus.

Its bark and leaves contain various bioactive compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Studies have shown that extracts from Amoora tsangii possess antimicrobial and antiviral activities, which may be useful in treating various infections.
